description_long: This object is a robust, rectangular bollard constructed with a red brick body and a thick, light gray concrete cap. The brickwork is laid in a traditional running bond pattern, giving it a classic urban appearance. The concrete cap is flat with a slightly raised central section, providing a subtle architectural detail. The base of the bollard is flush and appears to rest directly on the ground, with no visible anchoring hardware. The surface of the bricks is slightly rough and matte, while the concrete cap is smoother but still has a matte finish. This bollard is designed to serve as a sturdy access control barrier in urban environments, such as sidewalks, plazas, or building perimeters.
description: A rectangular red brick bollard with a thick, flat light gray concrete cap and a traditional urban design. The cap features a slightly raised central section, and the brick body is finished in a classic running bond pattern.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the red brick body and the flat, light gray concrete cap with its raised central section.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the uniform brick pattern and the thickness of the concrete cap, with no visible features differing from the front.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting a slightly lighter brick coloration and the same cap design, with subtle surface texture variations.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the brick pattern and cap thickness, with consistent design elements.
attribute_cap_design: flat with slightly raised central section
